label: Resend Contact Form
summary: Server-rendered contact form that submits to a Next.js Route Handler and sends the message as an email via Resend. Client component owns the form state + UX feedback (loading / success / error); server route owns the API call + validation. Degrades gracefully (mock: success): with no real RESEND_API_KEY (missing or an F2 preview stub) the route returns 200 { ok:true, demo:true } and the form shows a 'demo — not really sent' notice; a real key but missing EMAIL_FROM / CONTACT_EMAIL_TO returns 503 'email-not-configured' and the form renders a calm IntegrationConfigNotice.

envVars.purpose: Server-side Resend authentication. Find it in the Resend dashboard under API Keys. When missing or a preview stub the route returns a demo success (mock: success) instead of sending, so F3 build does not crash.
From-address used in outgoing emails. Must be a verified domain in Resend (e.g. 'Site <noreply@yourdomain.com>'). Same 503-fallback as RESEND_API_KEY.
Inbox that receives form submissions. Free-form email address; Resend does not need to verify it. Same 503-fallback as RESEND_API_KEY.
